heading: Petitions
label: 312 Petitions
notes: Amendments2011—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to determination of issue by Director.Pub. L. 112–29, § 6(a)Subsec. (a). , substituted “the information presented in the request shows that there is a reasonable likelihood that the requester would prevail with respect to at least 1 of the claims challenged in the request,” for “a substantial new question of patentability affecting any claim of the patent concerned is raised by the request,” and “A showing that there is a reasonable likelihood that the requester would prevail with respect to at least 1 of the claims challenged in the request” for “The existence of a substantial new question of patentability”.Pub. L. 112–29, § 6(c)(3)(A)(i)(I)Subsec. (c). , substituted “the showing required by subsection (a) has not been made,” for “no substantial new question of patentability has been raised,”.Pub. L. 112–29, § 6(c)(3)(A)(i)(II)2002—, made technical correction to directory language of , which enacted this section.Pub. L. 107–273, § 13202(c)(1)Pub. L. 106–113Subsec. (a). , struck out second sentence which read as follows: “On the Director’s initiative, and at any time, the Director may determine whether a substantial new question of patentability is raised by patents and publications.”Pub. L. 107–273, § 13202(a)(2)(A), inserted at end “The existence of a substantial new question of patentability is not precluded by the fact that a patent or printed publication was previously cited by or to the Office or considered by the Office.”Pub. L. 107–273, § 13105(a)Subsec. (b). , struck out “, if any” after “third-party requester”.Pub. L. 107–273, § 13202(a)(2)(B)

Effective Date of 2011 AmendmentAmendment by effective upon the expiration of the 1-year period beginning on , and applicable to any patent issued before, on, or after that effective date, with provisions for graduated implementation, see , set out as a note under .section 6(a) of Pub. L. 112–29section 6(c)(2) of Pub. L. 112–29section 311 of this titleSept. 16, 2011, (C), , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–29, § 6(c)(3)(B)125 Stat. 305Sept. 16, 2011“(B).—ApplicationThe amendments made by this paragraph [amending this section and ]—section 313 of this title“(i)shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act []; andSept. 16, 2011“(ii)shall apply to requests for inter partes reexamination that are filed on or after such date of enactment, but before the effective date set forth in paragraph (2)(A) of this subsection [set out as a note under ].section 311 of this title“(C).—Continued applicability of prior provisionsThe provisions of chapter 31 of title 35, United States Code, as amended by this paragraph [amending this section and ], shall continue to apply to requests for inter partes reexamination that are filed before the effective date set forth in paragraph (2)(A) as if subsection (a) [enacting and amending this section and sections 312 to 318 of this title] had not been enacted.”section 313 of this titlesection 319 of this title

L. 107–273section 13105(b) of Pub. L. 107–273section 303 of this titleNov. 2, 2002

L. 106–113section 41 of this title

children.text: the petition is accompanied by payment of the fee established by the Director under section 311;
the petition identifies all real parties in interest;

children.label: A
children.text: copies of patents and printed publications that the petitioner relies upon in support of the petition; and

children.label: B
children.text: affidavits or declarations of supporting evidence and opinions, if the petitioner relies on expert opinions;
the petition provides such other information as the Director may require by regulation; and
the petitioner provides copies of any of the documents required under paragraphs (2), (3), and (4) to the patent owner or, if applicable, the designated representative of the patent owner.

children.heading: .—Public Availability
children.label: b .—Public Availability
children.text: As soon as practicable after the receipt of a petition under section 311, the Director shall make the petition available to the public.
